content:
7.4 SEAL Template

Every prompt should end with an instruction to generate a SEAL block in a standard format, for example:

[HANDOFF_SEAL]
self_id:
world_ids: […]
character_ids: […]
depends_on: […]
emits: […]
next_prompt_path: <path under $PROMPT_ROOT or null>
notes:

────────────────────────
8. Automation Patterns via $AUTOMATION_ENGINE

8.1 Example Event: New World Declaration

Trigger: human or AI declares a new world and calls a “declare world” command.

$AUTOMATION_ENGINE should:

Create a new $ARCHIVE_ROOT/worlds/world-ID directory.
Generate a WORLD-MYTH-SPEC-CORE artifact via a $PROMPT_ROOT/world template.
Update $ARCHIVE_ROOT/meta/index_worlds.tsv.
Create a minimal repo under $REPO_ROOT/$ORG_ID/worlds (if needed) and initialize a feature branch feature/{world-id}-init.
Emit a SEAL describing what was created and which prompts to use next.
